content:
Consider the discrete-time system below with input $x[n]$ and output $y[n]$. In the figure, $h_1[n]$ and $h_2[n]$ denote the impulse responses of LTI Subsystems 1 and 2, respectively. Also, $\delta[n]$ is the unit impulse, and $b>0$.

Assuming $h_2[n] \neq \delta[n]$, the overall system (denoted by the dashed box) is_________.
explanation:
$$ \begin{array}{rlrl} \therefore p(n) & =\left[x(n) * h_1(n)\right]+b \delta(n) \\ \Rightarrow q(n) & =h_2(n) * p(n)=h_2(n) *\left[x(n) * h_1(n)+b \delta(n)\right] \\ & =x(n) * h_1(n) * h_2(n)+b h_2(n) \quad\left[\because h_2(n) * \delta(n)=h_2(n)\right] \\ \Rightarrow y(n) & =q(n)-b \delta(n) \\ & =\left[x(n) * h_1(n) * h_2(n)+b \cdot h_2(n)\right]-b \delta(n) \\ \therefore y(n) & =x(n) * h_1(n) * h_2(n)+\underbrace{b h_2(n)-b \delta(n)}_{f(n)} \end{array} $$

where, $\quad f(n)=b h_2(n)-b \delta(n)$

Because of $f(n)$ in the output $y(n)$, the system is non-linear and time-variant.

content: Consider a system with input $$x(t)$$ and output $$y(t) = x({e^t})$$. The system is
explanation:
Given : $y(t)=x\left(e^t\right)$

To check time invariance,

$$ \begin{aligned} & y\left(t, t_0\right)=T\left\{x\left(t-t_0\right)\right\}=x\left(e^t-t_0\right) .......(i) \\\\ & y\left(t-t_0\right)=x\left(e^{t-t_0}\right).........(ii) \end{aligned} $$

From equation (i) and (ii),

$$ y\left(t, t_0\right) \neq y\left(t-t_0\right) $$

So, given system is time variant

Let $t=3 \mathrm{sec}$

$$ y(3)=x\left(e^3\right) $$

According to above equation, output depends on future inputs.

So, given system is non-causal.

Hence, the correct option is (B).

content:
The output $y[n]$ of a discrete - time system for an input $x[n]$ is

$$ y[n]=\max\limits_{-\infty \leq k \leq n}|x[k]| $$

The unit impulse response of the system is
explanation:
Given : $y[n]=\max\limits_{-\infty \leq k \leq n}|x(k)|$

To find response of the system for impulse input, i.e. impulse response , taking

$$ \begin{aligned} x[n] & =\delta[n] \text { then output } \\ y[n] \cong & h[n]=\max _{-\infty \leq k \leq n}|\delta(k)| \\ h[n] & =0 \text { for } n<0 \\ & 1 \text { for } n \geq 0 \end{aligned} $$

As impulse will occur at $k=0$ and from the given condition $k \leq n$, so impulse response will be 1 for all $n \geq 0$, which is same as the definition of unit step signal $u[n]$.

Hence the correct option is (A).

options.content: It is causal but not stable
It is not causal but stable
It is neither causal nor stable

For a unit step input $u[n]$, a discreate-time $L T I$ system produces an output signal $(2 \delta[n+1]+\delta[n]+\delta[n-1])$. Let $y[n]$ be the output of the system for an input $\left(\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u[n]\right)$.

The value of $y[0]$ is The value of $y[0]$ is $\_\_\_\_$
explanation:
Solution: From question, we have Step response, $=S(n)$

Impulse Response, $\boldsymbol{h}[n]=S(n)-S(n-1)$

After putting the values, we get

$$ \begin{aligned} \boldsymbol{h}[n]= & 2 \delta[n+1]+\delta[n]+\delta[n-1] \\ & -2 \delta[n]-\delta[n-1]-\delta[n-2] \\ \boldsymbol{h}[n]= & 2 \delta[n+1]-\delta[n]-\delta[n-2] \end{aligned} $$

Now For the input value $x[n]=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u[n]$ :

Now the Output is,

$$ \begin{aligned} y[n]= & 2 \delta[n+1] *\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u[n]-\delta[n] *\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u[n] \\ & -\delta[n-2] *\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u[n] \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned}or,< & y[n]=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u(n+1)-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^n u(n)-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{n-2} u(n-2) \\ & y(0)=1-1=0 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \text { Hence, the correct answer is }(0 \text { to } 0) \text {. } $$

The transfer function of a discrete time LTI system is given by
$$H\left( z \right) = {{2 - {3 \over 4}{z^{ - 1}}} \over {1 - {3 \over 4}{z^{ - 1}} + {1 \over 8}{z^{ - 2}}}}$$

Consider the following statements:
S1: The system is stable and causal for $$ROC:\,\,\,\left| z \right| > \,1/2$$
S2: The system is stable but not causal for $$ROC:\,\,\,\left| z \right| < \,1/4$$
S3: The system is neither stable nor causal for $$ROC:\,\,1/4\, < \,\left| z \right| < \,{\raise0.5ex\hbox{$\scriptstyle 1$} \kern-0.1em/\kern-0.15em \lower0.25ex\hbox{$\scriptstyle 2$}}$$

Which one of the following statements is valid?

options.content: Both S1 and S2 are true
Both S2 and S3 are true
Both S1 and S3 are true
S1, S2 and S3 are all true

content: Consider the system shown in the Fig.1 below. The transfer function $$Y\left( z \right)/X\left( z \right)$$ of the system is

The output of a system is given in difference equation form as $$y\left( k \right) = \,a\,\,y\left( {k - 1} \right) + x\left( k \right),$$ where $$x\left( k \right)$$ is the input. If $$x\left( k \right)$$ $$\, = \,\,0$$ for $$k\, \ne \,0,\,\,x\left( 0 \right)\, = \,1,$$ and $$y\left( 0 \right)\, = \,0,$$ find $$y\left( k \right)$$ for all $$k.$$

Determine the range of $$'a'$$ for which $$y\left( k \right)\,$$ is bounded.

In Fig. 1, a linear time invariant discrete system is shown. Blocks labeled D represent unit delay elements. For $$n\, < 0,$$ you may assume that $$x\left( n \right),$$ $${y_1}\left( n \right),\,\,{y_2}\left( n \right)$$ are all zero.

(a) Find the expression for $${y_1}\left( n \right)$$ and $${y_2}\left( n \right)$$ in terms of $$x\left( n \right).$$
(b) Find the transfer function $${y_2}\left( z \right)/X\left( z \right)$$ in the $$z$$-domain.
(c) If $$x\left( n \right) = 1$$ at $$n = 0$$ or $$x\left( n \right) = 0$$ otherwise

Find $${y_2}\left( n \right).$$
answer:
(a) $$${y_1}\left( n \right) = x\left( {n - 1} \right) - 2{y_1}\left( {n - 1} \right) - {y_1}\left( {n - 2} \right)$$$ $$${y_2}\left( n \right) = x\left( {n - 2} \right) - 2{y_2}\left( {n - 1} \right) - {y_2}\left( {n - 2} \right)$$$

(b) $${{{y_2}\left( z \right)} \over {x\left( z \right)}} = {1 \over {{{\left( {z + 1} \right)}^2}}}$$

(c) $${y_2}\left( n \right) = 0$$ where $$n \le 0$$
